system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet straight duct (along +x)

    (  0  25 -25)   // 1
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 3
    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5 (same as 1)
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6 (same as 2)
    (-50 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend roof connecting block 0 to bend
    (  0  25  25)   // 8 (same as 4)
    (  0  25 -25)   // 9 (same as 5/1)
    ( 25  25 -25)   // 10
    ( 25  25  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - bend transition (goes down in -y)
    (  0 -25  25)   // 12 (same as 7)
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 13 (same as 6/2)
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 15

    // Block 3 - outlet straight duct extending in -y
    (  0 -125  25)  // 16
    (  0 -125 -25)  // 17
    ( 25 -125 -25)  // 18
    ( 25 -125  25)  // 19
    (-500 -50  25)  // 20
    (-500 -50 -25)  // 21
    (-500 -125 -25) // 22
    (-500 -125  25)  // 23
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight duct (along +x)
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend roof region
    hex (4 5 10 11 7 6 13 12) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Post-bend transition
    hex (16 17 18 19 12 13 14 15) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Outlet duct
    hex (20 21 22 23 16 17 18 19) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (20 21 22 23)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 1 5 4)   // front wall (+z)
            (0 3 2 1)   // back wall (-z)
            (1 2 6 5)   // left wall (-y)
            (4 5 6 7)   // right wall (+y)

            // Block 1 walls
            (4 8 11 7)  // left wall (-y)
            (5 6 10 9)  // right wall (+y)
            (8 9 10 11) // front wall (+z)
            (8 11 9 10) // back wall (-z)

            // Block 2 walls
            (12 13 14 15) // front wall (+z)
            (16 17 18 19) // back wall (-z)
            (12 13 16 17) // left wall (-y)
            (14 15 19 18) // right wall (+y)

            // Block 3 walls
            (20 21 23 22) // front wall (+z)
            (20 16 17 21) // back wall (-z)
            (20 23 17 22) // left wall (-y)
            (21 22 17 16) // right wall (+y)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (16 17 18 19 12 13 14 15) ... is inside-out`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Error 1 Deep Dive

The error is:
```
Block hex (16 17 18 19 12 13 14 15) ... is inside-out
```

This means the vertex ordering violates the right-hand rule. Let me trace the vertices:

- 16: (0, -125, 25)
- 17: (0, -125, -25)
- 18: (25, -125, -25)
- 19: (25, -125, 25)
- 12: (0, -25, 25)
- 13: (0, -25, -25)
- 14: (25, -50, -25)
- 15: (25, -50, 25)

The bottom face is `(16 17 18 19)` — all at y=-125 (flat horizontal plane)
The top face is `(12 13 14 15)` — vertices at y=-25 and y=-50 (sloping upward in y)

The problem is that the **normal from bottom to top face** is `(0, 100, 0)` (pointing in +y), but the bottom face `(16 17 18 19)` winds as (0,-125,25)→(0,-125,-25)→(25,-125,-25)→(25,-125,25). Using the right-hand rule on this face, the normal points in **+x direction**, not +y. So the block is inside-out.

### Recommended Fix

Reverse the vertex ordering of **Block 2** in `blockMeshDict`:

Change:
```
hex (16 17 18 19 12 13 14 15)
```
to:
```
hex (19 18 17 16 15 14 13 12)
```

This reverses the winding of both the bottom and top faces, flipping the normal direction so it now points outward correctly.

Alternatively, another equally valid approach is to shift the vertex ordering so the bottom face winds CCW when viewed from outside and the top face also winds CCW. Try:
```
hex (16 19 18 17 12 15 14 13)
```

Either reversal should resolve the "inside-out" error.

## Error 2

This is a cascading error from blockMesh failing. It will resolve automatically once blockMesh succeeds.

## Summary

1. **Modify Block 2** in `blockMeshDict` from `hex (16 17 18 19 12 13 14 15)` to `hex (19 18 17 16 15 14 13 12)` (reverse the vertex order).

2. **Update all boundary face definitions** in `blockMeshDict` to be consistent with the new block topology.

3. **Re-run `blockMesh`** before attempting `rhoSimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change Block 2 vertex ordering from hex (16 17 18 19 12 13 14 15) to hex (19 18 17 16 15 14 13 12) to fix inside-out block error'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
